title: bahiopsis chenopodina
intro: Bahiopsis chenopodina, also known as the desert marigold or desert sunray, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Bahiopsis chenopodina include reducing fever, alleviating digestive problems, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
